validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile on both new files exits 0 (0.14s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_scope_violation passes 16/16 (4.0s); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 160/160 up from 144 (27.8s)

checkpoint.summary: unit_022 complete. Scope-violation detector integrated into merge_batch.py as three new helpers -- _is_within_scope (fnmatch-based, recursive-glob-aware), _scope_violations (git merge-base + git diff --name-only, returns files matching no declared glob), _read_worktree_touches_paths (reads from WORKTREE_UNIT.json seeded by dispatch_batch). Per-unit loop now runs the scope check BEFORE _merge_unit; violators get status='failed', conflict={paths, category:'scope_violation'}, NO merge attempted, HEAD unchanged, branch preserved for inspection. Scope failures are always hard rejects (don't interact with conflict_strategy). Well-scoped peers still merge. 16 new tests (4 classes) cover helper semantics + three end-to-end rejection/partial-success/allow-through cases. Existing merge-conflict dicts now also carry category='merge_conflict' (strategy_applied preserved so unit_020 tests stay green). Test suite 144 -> 160.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_023 (PHASE_005): .harness/.lock file-based mutex around merge_batch.py using O_EXCL. Wrap merge_batch() invocations with acquisition at entry and release at exit (normal + exception paths). Contention case: second concurrent invocation blocks until first releases. Also document stale-lock detection (e.g., PID file contents; fallback on very old mtime).
